text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-defensive. The real driver for your names is Morgan Stanley flagging chip stocks as dangerously crowded — that's a direct headwind for your tech-heavy, margin book right now.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. PLTR is also flagged SELL, off 13% — two major losers bleeding simultaneously is dangerous when you're leveraged. SPAL is down nearly 17% with a leveraged decay problem on top; it's purely tactical and needs a hard look at that $19 stop today. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — margin amplifies any gap-down risk there.

Watch: 1) Whether the Morgan Stanley chip-crowding warning triggers more institutional selling in AVGO/SMH. 2) SpaceX bond news — $25B raise could pressure SPCX and SPAL as supply/dilution concerns circulate.

Bottom line: You have two large leveraged losers screaming SELL — protect capital now, not later.
